qa_pairs.question: How do I include a single command within a wiki page?
qa_pairs.answer: To include a command within the wiki, you should use the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template.

qa_pairs.question: What syntax does the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template detect?
Why are the "=" and "|" signs interpreted by the wiki when using the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template?
qa_pairs.answer: Since <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> is a template, the "=" and "|" signs are interpreted by the wiki.

qa_pairs.question: How can I include an equality sign within a command specified by the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template?
qa_pairs.answer: To include an equality sign, you must use the meta-template <nowiki>{{=}}</nowiki>.

qa_pairs.question: What is the correct way to include a pipe symbol within a command using the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template?
qa_pairs.answer: To include a pipe symbol, you should use <nowiki>{{!}}</nowiki>.

qa_pairs.question: Which template should I use to include a set of commands in the wiki?
qa_pairs.answer: You can use the <nowiki>{{Commands}}</nowiki> template to include a set of commands.

qa_pairs.question: How should individual commands be formatted when using the <nowiki>{{Commands}}</nowiki> template?
qa_pairs.answer: When using the <nowiki>{{Commands}}</nowiki> template, you may write each command on a separate line and prepend the '|' character in front of each command.

qa_pairs.question: How can I modify the command prompt displayed by the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template?
qa_pairs.answer: You can modify the command prompt by including a 'prompt' argument to the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template.

qa_pairs.question: How do I specify a custom prompt for a set of commands using the <nowiki>{{Commands}}</nowiki> template?
qa_pairs.answer: You can modify the command prompt for a set of commands by including a 'prompt' argument to the <nowiki>{{Commands}}</nowiki> template, such as `prompt=[name@briaree $]`.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to display the result of multiple commands using the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template?
qa_pairs.answer: No, you can display the result of only one command by adding the option <tt>result</tt> to the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template.

qa_pairs.question: What option allows me to display the output of a command using the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template?
qa_pairs.answer: You can display the result of a command by adding the option <tt>result</tt> to the <nowiki>{{Command}}</nowiki> template.
